summary: Auto causal-edge derivation is viable with adaptation. Deterministic graph-metadata and description fields should be promoted first with near-zero pollution risk; LLM-derived body-prose edges should run per chunk, require endpoint/relation/evidence/direction gates, and route medium-confidence proposals into a causal_edges_pending quarantine table.

claim: CocoIndex's conversation-to-knowledge design uses memoized per-session extraction, embedding-assisted entity resolution, canonicalization, and graph declaration, so the architecture maps to causal-edge derivation as staged target-state production rather than direct LLM graph writes.
evidence: conversation_to_knowledge/README.md:26-31 lists extraction, entity resolution, and graph storage
conversation_to_knowledge/README.md:95-102 says reruns skip unchanged sessions
conversation_to_knowledge/spec.md:42-49 says per-session processing is mounted and memoized
meeting_notes_graph_neo4j/main.py:400-438 mounts per-file processing, resolution, and relation declaration under component subpaths

claim: The conversation-to-knowledge design uses two LLM extraction calls per session, while the meeting-notes implementation uses one memoized LLM extraction per section plus LLM pair-resolution only for embedding-similar entity candidates.
evidence: conversation_to_knowledge/design.md:116-150 defines metadata and statement extraction models
conversation_to_knowledge/design.md:177-196 shows two memoized LLM steps per session
meeting_notes_graph_neo4j/main.py:198-211 memoizes extract_meeting per section
entity_resolution/__init__.py:281-297 calls resolve_pair only after candidate search

claim: CocoIndex deduplicates entity spellings by embedding each unique raw entity, searching FAISS top-N candidates above a similarity threshold, then asking an LLM pair resolver to select a match and canonical side.
evidence: entity_resolution/__init__.py:201-207 embeds sorted unique entities
entity_resolution/__init__.py:223-260 uses FAISS IndexFlatIP and thresholded top-N search
entity_resolution/__init__.py:296-337 applies PairDecision to create an acyclic dedup map
llm_resolver.py:30-46 instructs the LLM to return null when unsure

claim: CocoIndex's LLM pair resolver is structured and retried, but it does not emit calibrated per-edge confidence; our port must define confidence and quarantine semantics itself.
evidence: llm_resolver.py:22-28 response model has matched and canonical only
llm_resolver.py:106-123 retries invalid matched values and falls back to no match
entity_resolution/__init__.py:69-94 ResolutionEvent records decision/candidates/repointing but no confidence

claim: Our causal graph already has programmatic auto-edge support with strength and per-node caps, but public memory_causal_link remains manual and the schema lacks confidence/extraction_method columns.
evidence: causal-edges.ts:253-262 insertEdge accepts createdBy with default manual
causal-edges.ts:269-288 caps auto strength and rejects auto edges above per-node bounds
causal-graph.ts:668-736 validates manual link args and calls insertEdge without createdBy override
vector-index-schema.ts:607-621 defines strength/evidence/extracted_at/created_by but no confidence or extraction_method

claim: Relation vocabulary must be reconciled before LLM edge extraction because storage allows caused/enabled/supersedes/contradicts/derived_from/supports while stats-facing output still mentions produced and cited_by.
evidence: causal-edges.ts:18-25 defines live RELATION_TYPES
vector-index-schema.ts:613-615 enforces the same storage CHECK values
causal-graph.ts:112-119 includes produced and cited_by in MEMORY_CAUSAL_OUTPUT_RELATIONS

claim: Spec metadata already contains structured relationship fields that can be promoted without LLM extraction: parent_id, children_ids, manual.depends_on, manual.supersedes, manual.related_to, and phase children.
evidence: 013 graph-metadata.json:3-11 shows standard parent/children/manual relation slots
007 description.json:20-27 shows manual.depends_on and soft_depends_on
007 graph-metadata.json:7-18 shows normalized depends_on and related_to arrays
003 graph-metadata.json:6-28 shows children_ids plus object-form dependency reasons

claim: Existing graph health checks can detect skew and orphaning after the fact, but they are not sufficient acceptance gates for LLM-derived causal edges.
evidence: causal-graph.ts:140-181 computes relation skew over a time window
causal-graph.ts:824-870 reports coverage, relationCoverage, and orphanedEdges
feedback_*causal* search returned no prior causal-pollution feedback files
